pattern: Each element can shift within a fixed window, so the smallest achievable spread is the original spread shrunk by twice the window radius, never below zero.
hints: Only the largest and smallest values constrain the spread; everything in between can already be tucked inside whatever band they settle into.
Pull the maximum down by the most you are allowed and push the minimum up by the most you are allowed, then see what gap remains.
Compute (max - min) - 2*k and clamp the result at zero, since the bands of every element can be made to overlap once that quantity is non-positive.

edge_inputs.description: single element always collapses to zero
two elements with a window that only partly closes the gap
window large enough to fully equalize gives zero
zero window leaves the spread untouched
all equal values stay at zero regardless of k
huge window far exceeding the spread still floors at zero
exact half-spread window lands precisely on zero

stress.description: wide random array with a random window so both clamped and non-clamped cases occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 5000)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: covers both the floored-at-zero branch and the positive-residual branch
